what_it_does: Parses Python source under ~/company (read-only, stdlib ast) to build a symbol-to-symbol code-dependency graph: for each defined function/class it records which other known symbols the body calls or imports, producing code-edges.json with depends_on, depended_by, a stale honesty list, and bounded transitive reach queries. The standalone build is retired/superseded by ledger generation but kept as reference/fallback.
observations: Top docstring says the file is superseded as of 2026-06-27; code-edges.json is now generated from the ledger, and this module is a retired fallback.
Reuses symbols.symbol_id, symbols._stem, and refcheck.COMPANY/SEARCH_DIRS instead of inventing a parallel coordinate system.
Two-pass AST build: pass 1 parses every file and indexes defs/imports; pass 2 resolves references within each symbol's body.
Resolution precedence is same-file definition > explicit import > unambiguous global > ambiguous/unknown (no edge).
Stores only direct edges; transitive reach is computed at query time with a hard depth cap (2 or 3 via CODEEDGES_DEPTH).
reach_report explicitly signals when the depth bound is hit (capped:true) rather than silently truncating.
Unparseable or unreadable files become stale[] entries with resolves:false instead of being skipped or crashing.
Self-recursive references are ignored; unresolved ambiguous names are tallied in summary.unresolved_refs.
Deterministic output: sets are sorted before JSON serialization.
Provides a read seam (read parameter) so tests can inject inline fixtures without touching ~/company.
standouts: Retired/superseded status is prominently declared while the implementation remains runnable as fallback.
Precise resolution prevents a common name like run() from fanning out to all 18 definitions.
Bounded reach with explicit capped reporting is the explosion guard, not a precomputed closure.
Fail-loud stale list mirrors the honesty pattern used by refcheck/symbols.
conventions: Heavy explanatory docstrings and inline comments.
Configuration knob read from environment with a fail-loud range check.
Nested helper functions (ensure, resolve_name, resolve_attr) defined inside build_graph.
Uses ast.walk for traversal and sets converted to sorted lists for serialization.
Reuses sibling modules rather than reimplementing identifiers.
concerns: list_company_py claims to list every .py file under search_dirs but only scans os.listdir(base) one level deep, so subdirectories are missed.
DEPTH only permits 2 or 3, which may be too restrictive or require code changes for other bounds.
_imports_in handles ast.Import by referencing a.module, which does not exist on Import nodes; the fallback logic appears to work by accident but is confusing.
unresolved_refs is incremented per reference occurrence, so the tally may overcount repeated ambiguous names.
run_corpus writes code-edges.json even when stale entries exist, with no explicit warning in the output path.
notes: A retired but still-runnable AST-based builder that turns Python source into a precise, bounded, fail-loud symbol dependency graph keyed to the existing code:// coordinate system.
questions: Does the ledger-generated code-edges.json preserve the same schema and resolution semantics as this fallback?
Is this standalone script still exercised by tests, or is it purely documentation?
Is the one-level directory scan in list_company_py intentional given the comment says 'every .py file under'?
How are non-Python dependencies (e.g., templates, config, protobuf) represented in this graph?
What determines the choice between depth 2 and 3 in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds a symbol-to-symbol structural dependency graph. The only mismatch is the retirement notice, which says the file is now a fallback rather than the canonical generator.
apparent_themes: structural dependency graph over code symbols
intention_signals: The file is trying to add a relational layer on top of the existing symbol reverse-index so that consumers can ask 'what depends on this symbol?' and 'what does this symbol depend on?' without building an unbounded transitive closure or silently dropping bad data.
novelty: A precise symbol-level code-dependency graph with resolution precedence, a bounded capped reach query, and an explicit stale/unresolved honesty list, all keyed to a pre-existing code:// identifier scheme.
contribution: A concrete algorithm and fallback implementation for extracting direct symbol-to-symbol dependencies from Python ASTs, including ambiguity tallies, bounded transitive reach, and deterministic JSON output; also a pattern for fail-loud stale handling and read-seam injection for hermetic tests.
summary_for_embedding: Retired standalone AST builder that parses Python source under ~/company to produce a deterministic symbol-to-symbol code-dependency graph (code-edges.json) with direct depends_on/depended_by edges, bounded transitive reach, precise name resolution, and a fail-loud stale list.
intention_for_embedding: To add a relational structural layer over the existing symbol reverse-index so consumers can compute blast radius and respect dependencies without exploding the graph or silently hiding ambiguity.

symbols.does: Lists repo-relative .py paths under the configured search directories.
symbols.description: Returns a sorted, deterministic list of Python file paths under refcheck's SEARCH_DIRS within ~/company, skipping __pycache__; intended to feed the graph builder, though it currently scans only one directory level.

symbols.does: Reads a repo-relative source file from the company tree.
symbols.description: The on-disk read seam used by build_graph; returns file text or None if missing, enabling tests to replace it with inline fixtures.

symbols.does: Extracts function/class definitions from a parsed AST module.
symbols.description: Walks the AST and maps each defined name to its code://<stem>/<name> identifier, including nested defs and methods; first definition of a name wins.

symbols.does: Builds import alias maps from a module AST.
symbols.description: Produces mod_alias (import x [as y]) and name_from (from x import y [as z]) mappings from local name to module stem, used later to resolve imported symbols.

symbols.does: Collects names and attribute accesses referenced inside one symbol body.
symbols.description: Walks a single function/class AST node, returning bare names/Call targets and attribute-call pairs (base, attr) so they can be resolved to dependency edges.

symbols.does: Builds the full symbol-to-symbol dependency graph document.
symbols.description: Two-pass process: parse and index every file, then resolve each symbol's references into depends_on/depended_by edges using same-file, import, and unambiguous-global precedence; returns the code-edges.json document shape including edges, stale, summary, and shared.

symbols.does: Initializes an edge entry for a symbol id.
symbols.description: Nested helper that creates a default edges record with empty depends_on/depended_by sets and resolves:true whenever a symbol is first referenced.

symbols.does: Resolves a bare name to a target symbol id.
symbols.description: Nested helper applying resolution precedence: same-file definition, explicit import, unambiguous global; returns None for ambiguous or unknown names so they are tallied rather than fanned out.

symbols.does: Resolves attribute references like self.attr or module.attr.
symbols.description: Nested helper that treats self/cls as same-file, imported module aliases by stem, and otherwise falls back to resolving the attribute name as a bare name.

symbols.does: Computes bounded transitive dependencies of a start symbol.
symbols.description: BFS over depends_on up to the configured depth cap; returns the set of symbols the start depends on, excluding the start itself.

symbols.does: Reports bounded reach with explicit cap detection.
symbols.description: Like reach but returns a dict including reached symbols and capped:true when the graph continues past the depth limit, making truncation legible.

symbols.does: Self-checks the built graph document for basic shape.
symbols.description: Verifies that edges is a dict, every key is a code:// id, and each entry contains depends_on, depended_by, and resolves fields; raises ValueError on mismatch.

symbols.does: Runs the real corpus build and writes code-edges.json.
symbols.description: Lists company .py files, builds the graph, validates it, writes JSON to the module directory, and prints a summary including shared symbols and stale files.
Simple __main__ hook invoking the full corpus build and emit workflow.
